release_notes:
Alpha 4.1.1 - 2026-05-13 - TWO CRITICAL bugs in the enhance
pipeline (Pablo flagged on the EL Kenvue Workings output).

BUG 1 — GROSS column was EMPTY in the enhanced output.

Pablo's input file had NET + VAT filled but GROSS empty. The
enhancer never wrote a GROSS formula. Worse, when rule 5 fired
below the EUR 10k threshold, the OLD back-calc formula was
``=GROSS/(1+rate)`` — referencing the empty GROSS cell -> 0 ->
NET also turned to 0 -> cascading zeros into Summary / PIVOT
/ ESPL.

Fix: GROSS is now ALWAYS written as the live formula
``=NET+VAT`` (matches the SAP -> VATify writer's contract at
vatify_writer.py:679-686). The back-calc formula for NET is
now ``=VAT/rate`` (NOT ``=GROSS/(1+rate)``), so the formula
works regardless of whether GROSS was filled in the input.

Same fix applied to BOTH paths:
- src/vatify_enhancer.py (legacy)
- src/vatify_enhancer_fast.py (fast mode)

BUG 2 — Back-calc fired when the actual VAT/NET ratio matched
another valid country rate.

Pablo's scenario: SAP report says VAT rate 24% (Greek
standard) but the math actually gives 13% — the Greek reduced
rate. The OLD logic back-calc'd NET as if 24% were correct,
destroying the (already correct) NET amount.

Pablo's rule: "Si el ratio coincide con OTRO rate válido del
país, NO HACE FALTA RECÁLCULO. Solo un comentario indicando
que el VAT rate label no es el correcto."

Fix: new ``match_valid_rate(country, computed, declared,
tolerance)`` helper in src/mapping_engine.py returns the
matching alternative rate (or None). When rule 5 detects a
divergence, it now FIRST checks for an alternative match. If
found:
- Emit comment "VAT rate label is X% but the actual VAT/NET
ratio is Y% (also a valid rate in <COUNTRY>); NET/VAT
kept as-is, only the rate label is misleading"
- Skip the back-calc (NET stays as-is, GROSS stays as
NET+VAT).
Otherwise the legacy back-calc path runs (still triggered for
genuinely wrong VAT amounts).

Reduced-rate vocabulary is pulled from
``country_data.BAKED_FACTS`` (Standard + reduced rates per
country, e.g. GR -> [24%, 13%, 6%]). The GR/EL alias is
handled transparently.

Same bypass applied to BOTH paths:
- src/vatify_enhancer.py (_build_comment_for_row)
- src/transformer.py (_build_row, Rule 5)

BONUS FIX — latent NameError in transformer.py rule 5 back-calc.
The line ``_backcalc_rate = standard_rate_for(country)``
referenced ``country`` which is NOT a parameter of
``_build_row`` (the parameter is ``reporting_country``). The
NameError was swallowed by ``except Exception``, falling back
to 0.21 (Spanish rate) silently for every country. So the
back-calc has been using the WRONG divisor for years on
non-ES files. Now fixed to use ``reporting_country``.

Tests: +13 in tests/test_411_gross_and_alt_rate.py covering:
- valid_rates_for(GR/EL/ES/unknown) shape
- match_valid_rate three-way: matches alt / no match / skips
self
- enhancer alt-rate bypass (no back-calc) and genuine
back-calc (correct divisor 0.24, not 0.21)
- transformer alt-rate bypass + genuine back-calc parity
- GROSS formula written for every data row
- back-calc formula uses =VAT/rate (not =GROSS/(1+rate))

Verified end-to-end on Pablo's actual file
(20260513161231_invoices_11743521882844098372.xlsx, EL Kenvue):
- Row 511 (NET=2260, VAT=135.60, declared=24%, ratio=6%) ->
NET kept as-is, comment "VAT rate label is 24% but ratio is
6% (also a valid rate in EL); kept as-is".
- Row 455 (NET=2613.48, VAT=533.16, ratio=20.40%, NOT a GR
rate) -> NET back-calc'd as =G455/0.24 (correct divisor).
- All rows: GROSS = =E+G (the canonical VATify formula).

Suite: 766 passed, 5 skipped, 0 failures.
